prompt: Nightly snapshot rotation keeps wedging itself. The pattern from the cron logs: a night where there was nothing to prune, or where a bad retention value got rejected, and then EVERY later run dies with `LockHeldError: rotation already running in /srv/backups/db1` until somebody deletes .rotate.lock by hand. There is definitely no concurrent rotation — it's a single cron entry. Note the lock must keep doing its real job (a second rotation of the same directory while one is in flight has to be refused), so don't just rip it out. Repro: python3 test_rotate.py. Fix rotate.py and run the tests to confirm; leave test_rotate.py alone.
